segments.text: Hello, Memphis. I'm Ford Canale, Chairman of the Memphis City Council.
And I'm Chairwoman-Elect Jana Swearengen Washington. We're here bringing you the latest from our November 18th council meeting.

Make sure you follow us on social media at MemphisCityCouncil to stay updated on all the great things happening in our city. I'm excited to announce that our official council podcast, The Council Connect, is live. You can listen right now at wherever you get your podcasts.

We began the council day in the Public Safety and Homeland Security Committee. The Memphis Police Department provided an update on crime from October to date. I'm pleased to say crime is down in all categories.
It's Veterans and Military Families Month. During our regular council meeting, Councilman Spinoza sponsored a resolution honoring the sacrifice of service members and their families.
Councilwoman Cooper-Sutton also sponsored a resolution to First Baptist Church of Lauderdale, celebrating 160 years in ministry.
The council approved grant funding in the amount of 105,000 for maintenance repairs at David Carnes Park.
We also accepted $60,000 from the Tennessee Department of Disability and Aging for senior programming at the Josephine Lewis and Frazier Raleigh Senior Centers.
The council accepted more than $1.7 million from the Tennessee Department of Transportation for the Winchester Drive interchange at Perkins Road.
Lastly, we'd like to thank Pastor May Jr. For serving as our Chaplain of the Day.
Memphis, your city council is the forefront of ensuring transparent leadership and fostering collaboration between your elected officials and the communities we serve.
Thanks for tuning in and staying engaged. Don't forget, follow us on social media at MemphisCityCouncil on all platforms. Together we're building a stronger, safer Memphis in 2025.
